title: T-001 — customers + customer_contacts + customer_addresses + customer_allergen_restrictions schema (11-shipping-a)
prompt:
# T-001 — Customer-domain schema (P1 baseline)

PRD: docs/prd/11-SHIPPING-PRD.md §9.1 (customers, customer_contacts, customer_addresses tables), §6 D-SHP-7 RLS Multi-tenant (ADR-003/013), §11 V-SHIP-SO-02 shipping address required, §15.4 SHIP-003 Shipping Addresses, §15.4 SHIP-004 Allergen Restrictions per Customer
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Introduce the 4 customer-domain tables (`customers`, `customer_contacts`, `customer_addresses`, `customer_allergen_restrictions`) with `org_id` as canonical business-scope column, RLS + FORCE RLS using foundation `app.current_org_id()` function (T-125 `withOrgContext` HOF), R13 audit columns, and Drizzle ORM schemas. Read migrations/ to confirm there are no prior `customers` columns to preserve.

## Implementation contract
1. Read `packages/db/migrations/` to verify no prior customers/* schema exists; if any, document gap and abort.
2. New migration `packages/db/migrations/00XX_shipping_customers.sql` (next free 4-digit slot) creating:
   - `customers` (org_id uuid not null, site_id uuid nullable, customer_code text UNIQUE per org, name, email, phone, tax_id, category text CHECK in ('retail','wholesale','distributor'), allergen_restrictions JSONB DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb, credit_limit numeric(14,2) nullable (P2-gated by feature flag), is_active bool default true, R13 audit cols)
   - `customer_contacts` (org_id, customer_id FK ON DELETE CASCADE, name, title, email, phone, is_primary bool, R13)
   - `customer_addresses` (org_id, customer_id FK ON DELETE CASCADE, address_type text CHECK ('billing','shipping'), is_default bool, address_line1, address_line2 nullable, city, state nullable, postal_code, country_iso2 char(2), dock_hours jsonb nullable, notes text, R13)
   - `customer_allergen_restrictions` (org_id, customer_id FK ON DELETE CASCADE, allergen_id uuid references allergen_families (from 02-settings ref tables), restriction_type text CHECK ('refuses','requires_decl'), notes, R13)
3. ENABLE + FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY on all 4 tables. Policies `<table>_org_context` USING `(org_id = app.current_org_id())` WITH CHECK same. No `current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id')` GUC reads anywhere.
4. Add indexes: `customers(org_id, customer_code) UNIQUE`, `customer_addresses(org_id, customer_id, address_type)`, `customer_allergen_restrictions(org_id, customer_id, allergen_id) UNIQUE`.
5. Drizzle ORM schemas in `packages/db/src/schema/shipping/customers.ts` (export typed Insert/Select).
6. Register erasure handler stub via @monopilot/gdpr `registerErasureHandler('shipping_customers', fn)` (T-113 registry) — covers `customers.email`, `customers.phone`, `customers.tax_id`, `customer_contacts.email`, `customer_contacts.phone`.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/00XX_shipping_customers.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/shipping/customers.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/index.ts [modify — re-export shipping/customers]
- packages/db/__tests__/shipping-customers-schema.test.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/erasure/shipping-customers.ts [create] (registers handler with @monopilot/gdpr)

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a fresh DB, when the migration runs, then `\d customers` lists all columns (including `org_id NOT NULL`, R13 audit cols), and RLS + FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY are both enabled; same for the 3 sibling tables.
2. Given two distinct orgs A and B each have `app.set_org_context(session_token, org)` called as `app_user`, when each inserts a customer with the same customer_code='C-001' and then SELECTs from customers, then each sees only its own row (no cross-org leak; UNIQUE per org_id holds).
3. Given pg_policies is inspected, when policies for the 4 tables are listed, then qual/withcheck reference `app.current_org_id()` and contain no `current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id')` GUC reads.
4. Given the GDPR erasure handler is invoked for a `subject_id` matched to `customers.email` or `customer_contacts.email`, when the registered handler runs in a transaction, then the PII columns are nulled and an `audit_events` row with `retention_class='security'` is written (consumer of T-113 contract).

## Test strategy
- RED: `packages/db/__tests__/shipping-customers-schema.test.ts` asserts AC 1-4 before migration exists; uses testcontainers Postgres 16 with two parallel tx (T-125 pattern); grep-asserts policy text contains `app.current_org_id()` and contains no `current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id')`.
- `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test shipping-customers-schema`

## Risk red lines
- Do not name the business-scope column `tenant_id`; Wave0 v4.3 + foundation T-125 mandate `org_id`.
- Do not use `current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id')` GUC reads; use `app.current_org_id()` function (foundation T-125).
- Do not omit FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY.
- Do not add cascade DELETE on the org_id FK (orgs are immutable in this migration).
- Do not seed default allergen rows; that is owned by 02-SETTINGS §8 reference table.
- Do not implement `credit_limit` enforcement logic here — column exists but Phase 2 per §4.2.

pipeline_inputs.description: 4 customer-domain tables + RLS via app.current_org_id() + R13 audit + Drizzle schema + GDPR erasure handler registration.
pipeline_inputs.details: Pre-impl step: `git status --short` then read `packages/db/migrations/` directory to confirm next free 4-digit migration slot and that no prior customers/* schema exists. RLS strictly via foundation `app.current_org_id()` (T-125), never GUC reads. Wave0 v4.3 column name is `org_id`. R13 audit cols: created_at, created_by, updated_at, updated_by, deleted_at NULL. Erasure handler scope is PII columns only — never drops rows, only nulls.
out_of_scope: Do not implement Server Actions or APIs (T-002).
cross_module_dependencies: 01-npd/T-001 (product FG SSOT — variance_tolerance_pct + allergen attributes; coverage hygiene per R3 audit 2026-05-14)
acceptance_criteria: Given a fresh DB, when the migration runs, then \d customers/customer_contacts/customer_addresses/customer_allergen_restrictions show all columns including org_id NOT NULL + R13 audit cols, and RLS + FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY enabled on all 4 tables.

Given two orgs each call app.set_org_context per session as app_user, when each inserts customer with customer_code='C-001' and SELECTs, then each sees only its own row (no cross-org leak; UNIQUE per org_id holds).
Given pg_policies is inspected, when policies are listed, then qual/withcheck reference app.current_org_id() and contain no current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id') GUC reads.

Given the GDPR erasure handler is invoked for a subject_id, when it runs in a tx, then PII columns (email/phone/tax_id) are nulled and an audit_events row with retention_class='security' is emitted per T-113 contract.
test_strategy: RED: testcontainers Postgres 16 isolation test asserting AC 1-4 (RLS + cross-org isolation + pg_policies grep + erasure handler hook).
Run 'pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test shipping-customers-schema'.
risk_red_lines: Do not name the business-scope column tenant_id.
Do not omit FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY.
Do not add cascade DELETE on the org_id FK.
